title: Floral Fluffy Brazilian Rice Soup
description: A fragrant, light rice soup infused with subtle floral notes of hibiscus and rose water, simmered with apple cider for a hint of sweetness and a gentle, airy texture—perfect for a solo dinner that honors Brazilian warmth without the heaviness.

ingredients.name: Dried hibiscus tea
ingredients.notes: Steep in 1 cup water, then strain

ingredients.name: Rose water
ingredients.notes: Add at the end to preserve flavor

instructions: Rinse the jasmine rice under cold water until the water runs clear, then set aside.
In a medium pot, heat a splash of olive oil and sauté the minced garlic until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the rice to the pot and stir to coat, cooking for 1 minute to toast lightly.
Pour in the low‑sodium vegetable broth and apple cider, and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the rice is tender.
Meanwhile, steep the hibiscus tea in 1 cup hot water for 5 minutes, then strain out the petals.
Once the rice is cooked, stir in the hibiscus tea infusion, fresh lime juice, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Simmer uncovered for another 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the soup to become slightly thicker.
Remove from heat, add the rose water, and stir gently.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped cilantro, and enjoy your airy, floral Brazilian rice soup.
